Gulf Horizon Utilities PJSC — Operational and Cyber Incident Update under UAE PDPL context regarding legacy maintenance app access attempts.
On 28 May 2026, our SOC flagged repeated unauthorized logins to the onshore maintenance scheduler; we issued a public notice to Desert Exchange (DEX) and the Sands Capital Authority (SCA, fictional regulator) as routine market-abuse follow-up.
No impact on generation, billing systems, or previously announced ESG targets; this reiterates information already released via notice DEX/2026/114.
Attack vector involved phishing to the role mailbox maintenance@ghutilities.example and spoofed subdomains; logs show seven failed credential attempts and no elevation.
Captured legacy form labels from a staging screen included: Name:, Emirates ID:, DOB:, IBAN:, Device ID: — fields were blank or held placeholder values such as AE00 0000 0000 0000.
One contractor profile contained quasi-identifiers: DOB 1990-03-14, work location Al Ain depot, employer Gulf Horizon Utilities PJSC, role shift supervisor, device tag GHU-LAP-0772, payroll account ending 4482, family origin noted as Northern Emirates, masked Emirates ID 784-1990-xxxxxxx-0.
These elements were stored separately from names and were not paired with full identifiers; no complete Emirates ID, bank number, or residential address was exposed.
A vendor SFTP endpoint hosted in the Dinar International Financial Centre (free zone, fictional) was misconfigured; Orion Compliance Services FZ-LLC (fictional) remediated on 30 May and rotated access keys.
Our corporate trade licence reference on the website remains TL-9102773-X (public/stale and non-personal), and supplier registration fields displayed placeholders (e.g., REG-000-PLACEHOLDER).
No trading restrictions were requested; the DEX listing officer confirmed the disclosure is standard follow-up and not market-sensitive in its current form.
Placeholder strings like 784-0000-0000000-0 and 123-456-XYZ appear in diagnostic logs and do not map to real identifiers or accounts.
Generic descriptors noted in screenshots (mid-level staff, 30s, Gulf-based) are non-identifying and do not single out any individual on their own.
For queries, use security@ghutilities.example or 800-GHU-CARE (public helpline); please avoid sending personal documents to these channels.
A final root-cause and lessons-learned memo will be provided to the Sands Capital Authority and the free zone supervisor in alignment with UAE PDPL obligations and prior public disclosures.
